name: Deploy Zenith Trends to AWS

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  workflow_dispatch:

env:
  AWS_REGION: ap-southeast-2
  NODE_VERSION: '20'

jobs:
  deploy:
    name: Build & Deploy to S3 + CloudFront
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v2
        with:
          version: 10

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'pnpm'
          cache-dependency-path: 'frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml'

      - name: Configure AWS credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      # ── 1. Deploy infra first (so we get the API URL) ──────────
      - name: Install CDK dependencies
        working-directory: ./cdk
        run: npm install

      - name: Build CDK
        working-directory: ./cdk
        run: npm run build

      - name: Bootstrap CDK
        env:
          AWS_ACCOUNT_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}
        run: npx cdk bootstrap "aws://$AWS_ACCOUNT_ID/$AWS_REGION"

      - name: Deploy CDK Stack
        working-directory: ./cdk
        env:
          AWS_ACCOUNT_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}
        run: npx cdk deploy ZenithTrendsWebsiteStack --require-approval never --outputs-file outputs.json

      - name: Extract Stack Outputs
        id: stack-outputs
        working-directory: ./cdk
        run: |
          BUCKET_NAME=$(jq -r '.[].BucketName // empty' outputs.json)
          DISTRIBUTION_ID=$(jq -r '.[].DistributionId // empty' outputs.json)
          QUOTE_API_URL=$(jq -r '.[].QuoteApiUrl // empty' outputs.json)
          echo "bucket_name=$BUCKET_NAME" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "distribution_id=$DISTRIBUTION_ID" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "quote_api_url=$QUOTE_API_URL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      # ── 2. Build frontend with the API URL injected ────────────
      - name: Install frontend dependencies
        working-directory: ./frontend
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build Next.js static site
        working-directory: ./frontend
        env:
          NODE_ENV: production
          NEXT_PUBLIC_QUOTE_API_URL: ${{ steps.stack-outputs.outputs.quote_api_url }}
        run: pnpm run build

      # ── 3. Deploy static files ─────────────────────────────────
      - name: Sync static site to S3
        env:
          BUCKET_NAME: ${{ steps.stack-outputs.outputs.bucket_name }}
        run: |
          # Boost parallel uploads: 20 concurrent transfers (default is 10)
          aws configure set default.s3.max_concurrent_requests 20

          # Hashed assets (_next/static) — immutable, cache 1 year
          # These filenames contain content hashes so --size-only is safe
          aws s3 sync frontend/out/_next/static "s3://$BUCKET_NAME/_next/static" \
            --size-only \
            --cache-control "public, max-age=31536000, immutable"

          # Everything else (HTML pages, images, etc) — short cache
          # No --size-only here: content can change without size changing (e.g. color hex swaps)
          aws s3 sync frontend/out "s3://$BUCKET_NAME" \
            --delete \
            --exclude "_next/static/*" \
            --cache-control "public, max-age=60, s-maxage=3600"

      - name: Invalidate CloudFront cache
        if: steps.stack-outputs.outputs.distribution_id != ''
        env:
          DISTRIBUTION_ID: ${{ steps.stack-outputs.outputs.distribution_id }}
        run: |
          aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
            --distribution-id "$DISTRIBUTION_ID" \
            --paths "/*" \
            --no-cli-pager

      - name: Deployment summary
        env:
          BUCKET_NAME: ${{ steps.stack-outputs.outputs.bucket_name }}
          DISTRIBUTION_ID: ${{ steps.stack-outputs.outputs.distribution_id }}
          QUOTE_API_URL: ${{ steps.stack-outputs.outputs.quote_api_url }}
        run: |
          echo "Deployment successful!"
          echo "S3 Bucket: $BUCKET_NAME"
          echo "CloudFront: $DISTRIBUTION_ID"
          echo "Quote API: $QUOTE_API_URL"

#!/bin/bash

# Zenith Trends Website Runner
# This script sets up and runs the website locally

set -e

echo "🚀 Starting Zenith Trends Website..."
echo ""

# Kill any process on port 3000
if lsof -ti:3000 > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "🔄 Killing existing process on port 3000..."
    lsof -ti:3000 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null || true
    echo "✅ Port 3000 cleared!"
    echo ""
fi

# Check if pnpm is installed
if ! command -v pnpm &> /dev/null; then
    echo "❌ pnpm is not installed!"
    echo "📦 Installing pnpm..."
    npm install -g pnpm
    echo "✅ pnpm installed successfully!"
    echo ""
fi

cd frontend

# Check if node_modules exists
if [ ! -d "node_modules" ]; then
    echo "📦 Installing dependencies..."
    pnpm install
    echo "✅ Dependencies installed!"
    echo ""
fi

echo "🌐 Starting development server..."
echo "📍 Open http://localhost:3000 in your browser"
echo ""

pnpm dev

          DISTRIBUTION_ID=$(jq -r '.ZenithTrendsWebsiteStack.DistributionId // empty' outputs.json)

aws route53 list-resource-record-sets --hosted-zone-id Z0371626CPZP3KVXVDM9 --profile zenith --output json 2>&1 | python3 -c "
import json, sys

records = json.load(sys.stdin)['ResourceRecordSets']
hostinger_ip = '31.220.110.124'
deletions = []

for r in records:
    name = r['Name']
    rtype = r['Type']
    values = [rr.get('Value', '') for rr in r.get('ResourceRecords', [])]

    # Hostinger A records
    if rtype == 'A' and any(hostinger_ip in v for v in values):
        deletions.append({'Action': 'DELETE', 'ResourceRecordSet': r})
        print(f'  DELETE {name} {rtype} -> {values}')

    # Hostinger AAAA record
    if name == 'zenithtrends.com.au.' and rtype == 'AAAA' and any('2a02:4780' in v for v in values):
        deletions.append({'Action': 'DELETE', 'ResourceRecordSet': r})
        print(f'  DELETE {name} {rtype} -> {values}')

    # Hostinger CNAMEs
    if rtype == 'CNAME' and any('hostinger' in v for v in values):
        deletions.append({'Action': 'DELETE', 'ResourceRecordSet': r})
        print(f'  DELETE {name} {rtype} -> {values}')

    # www CNAME (CDK will create A alias to CloudFront instead)
    if name == 'www.zenithtrends.com.au.' and rtype == 'CNAME':
        deletions.append({'Action': 'DELETE', 'ResourceRecordSet': r})
        print(f'  DELETE {name} {rtype} -> {values}')

print(f'\nTotal records to delete: {len(deletions)}')
print(json.dumps({'Changes': deletions}))
" 2>&1

Before I delete these, to address your question — the **website stack** (S3, CloudFront, Lambda, API Gateway) deploys to **ap-southeast-2** as before. The only thing in **us-east-1** is the ACM certificate, which is a CloudFront requirement (CloudFront only accepts certificates from us-east-1). Route 53 is a global service, so it doesn't matter which region.

Your CDK `app.ts` already had this setup — I'm just wiring the certificate automatically instead of requiring you to copy-paste the ARN.

Here are the **6 old Hostinger records** I'm about to delete (these are NOT your Google Workspace records):

| Record | Type | Value | Why delete |
|--------|------|-------|------------|
| `zenithtrends.com.au` | AAAA | `2a02:4780:...` | Hostinger IPv6 |
| `\100.zenithtrends.com.au` | A | `31.220.110.124` | Hostinger IP |
| `ftp.zenithtrends.com.au` | A | `31.220.110.124` | Hostinger FTP |
| `autoconfig` | CNAME | `mail.hostinger.com` | Hostinger mail config |
| `autodiscover` | CNAME | `mail.hostinger.com` | Hostinger mail config |
| `www` | CNAME | `zenithtrends.com.au` | CDK replaces with A alias to CloudFront |

**Preserved** (untouched):
- MX records (Google Workspace)
- SPF/TXT records
- DMARC record
- SES DKIM records
- NS/SOA records

Shall I proceed with deleting these 6 records and then deploying?

| What | Status |
|------|--------|
| ACM Certificate (us-east-1) | Created + auto-validated via Route 53 |
| CloudFront | Custom domain `zenithtrends.com.au` + `www` attached with SSL |
| Route 53 A records | Apex + www → CloudFront (alias) |

| SPF/DMARC/DKIM | Preserved (untouched) |
| Old Hostinger records | Cleaned up (6 records deleted) |
| CI/CD | Updated — `cdk deploy --all` deploys both stacks automatically |

**Going forward**: You just push code to `main` and GitHub Actions handles everything — CDK infra, frontend build, S3 sync, CloudFront invalidation. No manual steps needed.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Deploy Zenith Trends to AWS

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  workflow_dispatch:

env:
  AWS_REGION: ap-southeast-2
  NODE_VERSION: '20'

jobs:
  deploy:
    name: Build & Deploy to S3 + CloudFront
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v2
        with:
          version: 10

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'pnpm'
          cache-dependency-path: 'frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml'

      - name: Configure AWS credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      # ── 1. Deploy infra (cert + website stacks) ──────────────────
      - name: Install CDK dependencies
        working-directory: ./cdk
        run: npm install

      - name: Build CDK
        working-directory: ./cdk
        run: npm run build

      - name: Bootstrap CDK (both regions)
        env:
          AWS_ACCOUNT_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}
        run: |
          npx cdk bootstrap "aws://$AWS_ACCOUNT_ID/$AWS_REGION"
          npx cdk bootstrap "aws://$AWS_ACCOUNT_ID/us-east-1"

      - name: Deploy all CDK Stacks
        working-directory: ./cdk
        env:
          AWS_ACCOUNT_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}
        run: npx cdk deploy --all --require-approval never --outputs-file outputs.json

      - name: Extract Stack Outputs
        id: stack-outputs
        working-directory: ./cdk
        run: |
          BUCKET_NAME=$(jq -r '.ZenithTrendsWebsiteStack.BucketName // empty' outputs.json)
          DISTRIBUTION_ID=$(jq -r '.ZenithTrendsWebsiteStack.DistributionId // empty' outputs.json)
          QUOTE_API_URL=$(jq -r '.ZenithTrendsWebsiteStack.QuoteApiUrl // empty' outputs.json)
          echo "bucket_name=$BUCKET_NAME" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "distribution_id=$DISTRIBUTION_ID" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "quote_api_url=$QUOTE_API_URL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      # ── 2. Build frontend with the API URL injected ───────────────
      - name: Install frontend dependencies
        working-directory: ./frontend
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build Next.js static site
        working-directory: ./frontend
        env:
          NODE_ENV: production
          NEXT_PUBLIC_QUOTE_API_URL: ${{ steps.stack-outputs.outputs.quote_api_url }}
        run: pnpm run build

      # ── 3. Deploy static files ────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Sync static site to S3
        env:
          BUCKET_NAME: ${{ steps.stack-outputs.outputs.bucket_name }}
        run: |
          aws configure set default.s3.max_concurrent_requests 20

          aws s3 sync frontend/out/_next/static "s3://$BUCKET_NAME/_next/static" \
            --size-only \
            --cache-control "public, max-age=31536000, immutable"

          aws s3 sync frontend/out "s3://$BUCKET_NAME" \
            --delete \
            --exclude "_next/static/*" \
            --cache-control "public, max-age=60, s-maxage=3600"

      - name: Invalidate CloudFront cache
        if: steps.stack-outputs.outputs.distribution_id != ''
        env:
          DISTRIBUTION_ID: ${{ steps.stack-outputs.outputs.distribution_id }}
        run: |
          aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
            --distribution-id "$DISTRIBUTION_ID" \
            --paths "/*" \
            --no-cli-pager

      - name: Deployment summary
        run: |
          echo "Deployment successful!"
          echo "Website: https://zenithtrends.com.au"
